seoTitle: How to Receive Money on PayPal Without a Bank (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to accept and hold incoming payments in your PayPal balance using just your email or phone number, no bank account required.
intro: You don't need a linked bank account to receive money on PayPal. This guide shows how to confirm your contact details and start accepting transfers straight into your PayPal Balance. It's for anyone who wants to get paid without connecting traditional banking information.

steps.heading: Confirm your email address
steps.body: Go to Settings, find your registered email, and click Confirm to trigger the verification email.

steps.heading: Enter the verification code
steps.body: Check your inbox for the 6-digit code and paste it into PayPal to confirm your address.

steps.heading: Complete identity verification prompts
steps.body: Provide your legal name and stored address as requested to satisfy basic KYC requirements.

steps.heading: Activate your PayPal Balance Account
steps.body: Make sure your account is set up to hold incoming funds directly in a Balance Account container.

steps.heading: Receive and use your funds
steps.body: Once a payment arrives, it settles directly into your balance, which you can spend online or optionally link to a prepaid or debit card later.

faq.question: Can senders pay me with just my email address?
faq.answer: Yes, once your email is confirmed, others can send money using that address without needing your bank details.

faq.question: What can I do with my balance if I have no bank account?
faq.answer: You can spend it directly at PayPal-accepting merchants online, or link a prepaid or debit card for more flexibility.

faq.question: Will international payments work without a bank account?
faq.answer: Yes, though currency conversion settings may need adjusting to avoid holds on incoming international transfers.

faq.question: Is a bank account ever required later?
faq.answer: Adding a bank account can unlock features like direct withdrawal but isn't required just to receive and spend funds.
